title: Yamabe flow of asymptotically flat metrics
speaker: Yi Wang
abstract: In this talk, we will discuss the behavior of the Yamabe flow on an asymptotically flat (AF) manifold. We will first show the long-time existence of the Yamabe flow starting from an AF manifold and discuss the uniform estimates on manifolds with positive Yamabe constant. This would allow us to prove global weighted convergence along the Yamabe flow on such manifolds. We also prove that the flow will diverge if the Yamabe constant is nonpositive. It turns out that the blowup behavior of the flow starting from manifolds with nonpositive Yamabe constant is explicit.

proof_stages.label: Yamabe problem and Yamabe flow
proof_stages.summary: The talk reduces the constant-scalar-curvature problem in a conformal class to a nonlinear PDE and explains Hamilton's Yamabe flow as a heat-type/gradient-flow approach, with compact-manifold results as motivation.

proof_stages.label: Asymptotically flat framework, ADM mass, and weighted spaces
proof_stages.summary: The noncompact setting is specified by asymptotic flatness and decay assumptions, ADM mass and the positive mass theorem are recalled, and weighted Sobolev/Hölder spaces are introduced for global analysis.

proof_stages.label: Flow questions at infinity via Ricci-flow comparison
proof_stages.summary: Known Ricci-flow behavior on asymptotically flat manifolds and known short-time Yamabe-flow preservation results are compared in order to isolate the long-time-existence and convergence questions for the Yamabe flow.

proof_stages.label: Unconditional long-time existence
proof_stages.summary: The first main theorem states that the asymptotically flat Yamabe flow exists for all time without curvature hypotheses, supported analytically by finite-time control inequalities.

proof_stages.label: Positive-Yamabe convergence to a scalar-flat limit
proof_stages.summary: After defining the asymptotically flat Yamabe constant, the speaker states that positivity is exactly the convergence case, with global weighted convergence to a scalar-flat metric proved using uniform Sobolev control, the scalar-flat equivalence, Moser iteration, and weighted elliptic/parabolic estimates.

proof_stages.label: Nonpositive-Yamabe divergence and rescaled profiles
proof_stages.summary: When the Yamabe constant is nonpositive the flow diverges: for negative Yamabe constant there is a precise rescaled positive profile solving a constant negative scalar-curvature equation, while the critical zero case currently yields weaker sequential/profile information.

proof_stages.label: ADM mass gap and scalar-flat minimization
proof_stages.summary: In the convergent positive-Yamabe case, the flow gives a nonnegative ADM mass gap formula, showing that the scalar-flat representative minimizes ADM mass in its conformal class and relating this flow formula to earlier elliptic formulas.

examples.name: Dimension-two uniformization via Yamabe flow
examples.note: The two-dimensional case is mentioned as an application where the flow gives another proof of uniformization, illustrating the canonical-metric motivation before the noncompact AF problem.

examples.name: Graph hypersurface formula for ADM mass
examples.note: In response to a question about why the ADM flux is a mass, the speaker describes the special case of an asymptotically flat manifold that is a graph, where the mass can be written as a weighted scalar-curvature integral.
examples.engagement_reason: he states the known special graph formula for ADM mass as an intuition, but does not derive it or compute it for a graph

examples.name: Plane with a handle under Ricci-flow surgery
examples.note: An audience question about a plane with a handle is used to illustrate how Ricci-flow singularities can be removed by surgery while the ADM mass, being measured at infinity, is not affected.
examples.engagement_reason: he reasons through the plane-with-handle scenario under Ricci-flow surgery, describing the handle singularity, removal, convergence, and why the ADM mass is unaffected
